message.content:
Review a single new file for the Marketify project: `supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql`. It is the only change in this diff.

Context:
- Story US-012 acceptance criteria (verbatim):
  - Migration `us_012_seed` inserts: 1 lister with email, 1 listing in status=active with both pre and post conditions, 1 creator with tiktok_handle + instagram_handle and fake metric_snapshots covering all three scrape_modes
  - Verify via `execute_sql` that creator_profiles denorm columns reflect the seeded snapshots
- Authoritative specs:
  - `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 (schema) and §15 (triggers)
  - Existing migrations `supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql`, `20260417100000_us_005_listing_tables.sql`, `20260417120000_us_007_metric_snapshots.sql`, `20260417150000_us_010_cascade_trigger.sql`
- Hard constraints:
  - Migrations are append-only (never edit a prior one).
  - The US-010 BEFORE UPDATE cascade trigger fires on any UPDATE to `public.listings`; the seed deliberately inserts the listing as `active` first, then only touches `current_version_id` in a follow-up UPDATE so the trigger's versioned-field diff detects nothing and no cascade fires.
  - The US-007 BEFORE INSERT `denorm_metrics` trigger is expected to mirror the three metric_snapshots rows into `public.creator_profiles`. Verified via `execute_sql` post-apply: `tiktok_follower_count=42000`, `tiktok_avg_views_last_10=18500`, `tiktok_total_likes=850000`, `tiktok_video_count=120`, `tiktok_is_verified=false`, `instagram_follower_count=15000`, `instagram_avg_views_last_10=6400`, `instagram_media_count=55`, `metrics_fetched_at` set.

What I want from you:
- Confirm the seed is spec-compliant and consistent with existing migrations.
- Flag any real bugs, FK violations, missing columns, wrong enum values, or trigger interactions I may have missed.
- Flag any concern Medium or higher that should block commit.
- Purely cosmetic suggestions are fine but should be labeled as such.

Report only issues rated Medium or higher as blocking. Be concise.

content.text: I'll review the seed migration file and the relevant context files simultaneously.

content.content:
1	-- US-012 — Dev seed data per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7.
2	-- Creates: 1 lister, 1 active listing (v1 + pre/post conditions + sample video),
3	-- 1 creator with tiktok + instagram handles, and one metric_snapshots row for
4	-- each of the three scrape_modes (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts).
5	-- The app_private.denorm_metrics BEFORE-INSERT trigger (US-007) mirrors the
6	-- snapshots into public.creator_profiles, so no manual denorm updates here.
7	-- Fixed UUIDs so later stories (US-038 discover feed, US-055 edit campaign,
8	-- US-038+ verification flows) can reference the seeded rows deterministically.
9	
10	do $$
11	declare
12	  v_lister_id  uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111001';
13	  v_creator_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002';
14	  v_listing_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111010';
15	  v_version_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111011';
16	  v_tt_link_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020';
17	  v_ig_link_id uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111021';
18	begin
19	  -- ---- lister ----------------------------------------------------------
20	  insert into public.users (id, role, username, email)
21	  values (v_lister_id, 'lister', 'acme_studio', 'lister@seed.marketify.test');
22	
23	  insert into public.lister_profiles (user_id, org_name, website_url, description)
24	  values (
25	    v_lister_id, 'Acme Studio', 'https://acme.example',
26	    'Seed lister for development and RLS verification.'
27	  );
28	
29	  -- ---- creator ---------------------------------------------------------
30	  insert into public.users (id, role, username, email)
31	  values (v_creator_id, 'creator', 'seed_creator', 'creator@seed.marketify.test');
32	
33	  insert into public.creator_profiles (user_id, display_name, bio, country)
34	  values (v_creator_id, 'Seed Creator', 'Dev seed creator.', 'US');
35	
36	  insert into public.social_links (
37	    id, user_id, platform, handle, status, handle_confirmed_at
38	  ) values
39	    (v_tt_link_id, v_creator_id, 'tiktok',    'seed_creator_tt', 'linked', now()),
40	    (v_ig_link_id, v_creator_id, 'instagram', 'seed_creator_ig', 'linked', now());
41	
42	  -- ---- listing + v1 ----------------------------------------------------
43	  -- Insert as active immediately; current_version_id is wired after v1 exists.
44	  -- The follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id, so the US-010
45	  -- cascade trigger detects no versioned change and does not re-bump.
46	  insert into public.listings (
47	    id, lister_id, status,
48	    title, description, category,
49	    price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
50	    min_followers_tiktok, min_followers_instagram,
51	    published_at
52	  ) values (
53	    v_listing_id, v_lister_id, 'active',
54	    'Promote Acme Headphones',
55	    'Create a 30-60s short demonstrating our new headphones. See samples for vibe.',
56	    'tech',
57	    15000, 'USD', 10,
58	    1000, 500,
59	    now()
60	  );
61	
62	  insert into public.listing_versions (
63	    id, listing_id, version_number,
64	    price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
65	    snapshot, changed_fields
66	  ) values (
67	    v_version_id, v_listing_id, 1,
68	    15000, 'USD', 10,
69	    jsonb_build_object(
70	      'title',           'Promote Acme Headphones',
71	      'price_cents',     15000,
72	      'currency',        'USD',
73	      'max_submissions', 10
74	    ),
75	    array[]::text[]
76	  );
77	
78	  update public.listings
79	     set current_version_id = v_version_id
80	   where id = v_listing_id;
81	
82	  -- ---- pre + post conditions for v1 -----------------------------------
83	  insert into public.listing_conditions (
84	    listing_version_id, kind, metric, platform, operator, numeric_threshold
85	  ) values
86	    (v_version_id, 'pre', 'min_followers', 'tiktok',    'gte', 1000),
87	    (v_version_id, 'pre', 'min_followers', 'instagram', 'gte',  500);
88	
89	  insert into public.listing_conditions (
90	    listing_version_id, kind, metric, operator, bool_threshold
91	  ) values
92	    (v_version_id, 'post', 'post_family_friendly', 'bool', true);
93	
94	  insert into public.listing_conditions (
95	    listing_version_id, kind, metric, operator, text_threshold
96	  ) values
97	    (v_version_id, 'post', 'post_must_mention', 'contains', '#AcmeHeadphones');
98	
99	  insert into public.sample_videos (
100	    listing_version_id, platform, url, caption, sort_order
101	  ) values (
102	    v_version_id, 'tiktok',
103	    'https://www.tiktok.com/@acme_studio/video/7100000000000000001',
104	    'Reference vibe', 0
105	  );
106	
107	  -- ---- metric_snapshots (all three scrape_modes) -----------------------
108	  -- Triggers fire on INSERT: coherence check + denorm_metrics mirrors into
109	  -- creator_profiles. The three rows below populate the full denorm column
110	  -- set (tiktok_*, instagram_follower_count, instagram_media_count,
111	  -- instagram_avg_views_last_10, metrics_fetched_at).
112	  insert into public.metric_snapshots (
113	    social_link_id, scrape_mode, status,
114	    follower_count, avg_views_last_10, total_likes, video_count, is_verified,
115	    fetched_at
116	  ) values
117	    (v_tt_link_id, 'tiktok_profile', 'fresh',
118	     42000, 18500, 850000, 120, false, now()),
119	    (v_ig_link_id, 'ig_details', 'fresh',
120	     15000, null,  null,   55,   true,  now()),
121	    (v_ig_link_id, 'ig_posts', 'fresh',
122	     null,  6400,  null,   null, null,  now());
123	end $$;
124

content.content:
18:| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
65:| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
311:          scrape_mode: 'ig_details',       // or 'ig_posts' | 'tiktok_profile'
330:| Denormalized field on `creator_profiles` | TikTok source | Instagram source |
341:Stored fully in `metric_snapshots.raw_payload` (jsonb) for forensics and future backfills — if we later need `diggCount` trends we don't need to re-scrape.
348:[Omitted long matching line]
355:- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
382:| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension; denormalized metrics for fast filtering. |
384:| `social_links` | Creator's linked handles (TikTok and/or IG). No tokens — we don't OAuth. |
385:| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link, one Apify run per row. |
387:| `listing_versions` | Immutable versioned snapshots. |
388:| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows. |
413:create type scrape_mode as enum ('tiktok_profile', 'ig_details', 'ig_posts');
431:- `creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, `_avg_views_last_10`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from latest `metric_snapshots` via trigger. Enables O(log n) feed filtering.
432:- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, avoids feed join.
459:| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
496:-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized)
498:create table public.creator_profiles (
515:create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
516:create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);
532:-- 5. social_links   (no OAuth fields — Apify-based)
534:create table public.social_links (
555:create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
556:  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';
557:create unique index social_links_platform_handle_uniq
558:  on public.social_links (platform, handle) where status <> 'unlinked';
565:-- 6. metric_snapshots (historized, one per Apify run)
567:-- TikTok: 1 row per refresh (scrape_mode='tiktok_profile' — contains followers + avg views).
568:-- Instagram: 2 rows per refresh (scrape_mode='ig_details' for followers, scrape_mode='ig_posts' for avg views).
569:-- The denorm trigger updates only the columns owned by each scrape_mode.
570:create table public.metric_snapshots (
572:  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
573:  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
586:  -- scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform coherence enforced by trg_metric_snapshots_coherence below.
588:create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
589:-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode). So IG has at most two latest rows.
590:create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
591:  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;
592:create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
593:  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
596:create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
597:  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';
645:create table public.listing_versions (
653:  previous_version_id uuid references public.listing_versions(id),  -- for diff rendering
658:create index on public.listing_versions (listing_id, version_number desc);
661:  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);
664:-- 8. listing_conditions (data-driven)
666:create table public.listing_conditions (
668:  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
678:create index on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);
685:  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
699:  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete restrict,
825:alter table public.creator_profiles enable row level security;
827:alter table public.social_links enable row level security;
828:alter table public.metric_snapshots enable row level security;
830:alter table public.listing_versions enable row level security;
831:alter table public.listing_conditions enable row level security;
853:--   3. Listers see applicants' *public* metrics (via `creator_profiles.public_fields`)
869:-- ---------------- creator_profiles ----------------
870:create policy creator_profiles_self_rw on public.creator_profiles
873:create policy creator_profiles_lister_read on public.creator_profiles
878:      where a.creator_id = creator_profiles.user_id
889:-- ---------------- social_links ----------------
890:create policy social_links_self_rw on public.social_links
893:create policy social_links_lister_read on public.social_links
898:      where a.creator_id = social_links.user_id
903:-- ---------------- metric_snapshots ----------------
905:create policy metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
909:      select 1 from public.social_links sl
910:      where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
914:create policy metric_snapshots_lister_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
918:      select 1 from public.social_links sl
921:      where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
934:-- ---------------- listing_versions ----------------
935:create policy listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable on public.listing_versions
939:      where l.id = listing_versions.listing_id
945:-- ---------------- listing_conditions ----------------
946:create policy listing_conditions_read_if_version_readable on public.listing_conditions
949:      select 1 from public.listing_versions v
951:      where v.id = listing_conditions.listing_version_id
961:      select 1 from public.listing_versions v
1090:-- 1. Creator A cannot SELECT Creator B's social_links, metric_snapshots,
1104:-- edge function PATCH /listings/:id after it has applied listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1105:-- The edge function is the sole writer to listing_conditions + sample_videos once a listing is active;
1120:  -- to indicate listing_conditions or sample_videos were edited:
1130:    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot, previous_version_id, changed_fields)
1161:-- Helper the edge function calls after committing listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1167:--     2. `insert into public.listing_versions (...) returning id into v_new;`
1169:--     3. `insert into public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id=v_new, ...)` for each pre/post-condition.
1177:--        `refresh_listing_thresholds()` trigger on `listing_conditions` becomes
1185:--   The `refresh_listing_thresholds()` trigger on listing_conditions is therefore
1205:-- Coherence: scrape_mode must match the linked social_link's platform.
1211:  select platform into p from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
1215:  if (p = 'tiktok'    and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile')
1216:  or (p = 'instagram' and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details', 'ig_posts')) then
1217:    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
1222:create trigger trg_metric_snapshots_coherence
1223:  before insert or update of scrape_mode, social_link_id on public.metric_snapshots
1227:-- Fired BEFORE INSERT so we hold a row-level lock on social_links via advisory lock
1228:-- for the (social_link, scrape_mode) pair. This ensures concurrent webhook redeliveries
1230:create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
1233:  sl     public.social_links%rowtype;
1242:  -- Serialize per (social_link_id, scrape_mode). hashtext gives a stable bigint mapping.
1243:  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
1246:  -- Demote any previous latest for this (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
1247:  update public.metric_snapshots
1250:     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
1256:  -- Denormalize into creator_profiles — only columns owned by this scrape_mode.
1257:  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
1259:  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
1260:    update public.creator_profiles
1270:  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
1271:    update public.creator_profiles
1278:  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
1279:    update public.creator_profiles
1289:create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
1290:  before insert on public.metric_snapshots
1291:  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();
1353:-- threshold across the listing's CURRENT version. Fired on any write to listing_conditions.
1366:    join public.listing_versions lv on lv.listing_id = l.id
1386:             from public.listing_conditions
1394:             from public.listing_conditions
1406:create trigger trg_listing_conditions_refresh_thresholds
1407:  after insert or update or delete on public.listing_conditions
1419:               from public.listing_conditions
1425:               from public.listing_conditions
1506:  update public.metric_snapshots
1516:  update public.metric_snapshots
1566:  social_links: { id: string; platform: Platform; handle: string; status: 'linked' }[];
1570:[Omitted long matching line]
1575:- If it throws (rate limit, 4xx/5xx from Apify) → UPDATE snapshot to `failed` with `error_message`, increment `social_links.fail_count`, return normally. The UI chips the failed mode as "Couldn't fetch" with a retry button.
1605:Side effects: insert `social_links` + kick initial Apify run (async, webhook-completed).
1632:Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1634:| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
1644:- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
1668:  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
1675:Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).
1678:1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
1681:4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
1684:   update public.metric_snapshots
1695:6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
1696:7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.
1698:**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)
1824:| `metric_snapshots:social_link_id=<id>` | creator | INSERT, UPDATE (push metrics-ready) |
1861:- **Client:** on app resume, loads `creator_profiles` into context. Feed inlines `pre_conditions` per listing; filters locally. Instant "eligible only" toggle.
1889: │                                                  │─INSERT social_links (tt, ig)───────>│
1890: │                                                  │─INSERT metric_snapshots refreshing ×3
1915: │                                                                                 │─UPDATE metric_snapshots fresh─>DB
1916: │                                                                                 │   (trg denorm → creator_profiles)
1917: │  Realtime push metric_snapshots INSERT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────│
1929: │                                              │─SELECT creator_profiles─────│
1944: │                              │            ├─INSERT listing_versions │
1976: │                               │ For each scrape_mode owed by platform:         │
1978: │                               │                   scrape_mode=X)──>DB          │
1980: │                               │               webhook{scrape_mode:X,           │
1988: │                                                    │  parse by scrape_mode         │
1990: │                                                    │   (trg denorm_metrics branches│
1991: │                                                    │    on scrape_mode, updates    │
1993: │ Realtime push metric_snapshots UPDATE────────────────────────────────────────────│
2026:**Ready for build.** Step 1 is the migration from §4.7; step 2 is Apify client + webhook receiver edge function; step 3 is signup flows. See `product-plan.md` §6 for the full build sequence.

1	-- US-004 — Identity tables per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7
2	-- Tables: users, creator_profiles, lister_profiles, social_links.
3	-- RLS is intentionally NOT enabled here; it is added in US-009 (a single
4	-- `rls_policies` migration covering every user-data table at once). The
5	-- us_004 story explicitly allows the `rls_disabled_in_public` advisor to
6	-- remain until US-009 lands.
7	-- citext is pinned to the `extensions` schema by us_003 — qualify explicitly
8	-- because the default search_path does not include it.
9	-- Append-only: do not edit this file in place; later stories add migrations.
10	
11	-- ---- users --------------------------------------------------------------
12	create table public.users (
13	  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
14	  role       public.user_role not null,
15	  username   extensions.citext not null unique,
16	  email      extensions.citext unique,
17	  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
18	  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
19	  deleted_at timestamptz
20	);
21	create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;
22	
23	-- ---- creator_profiles ---------------------------------------------------
24	-- Denormalized metric columns mirror the latest public.metric_snapshots rows
25	-- per scrape_mode; the mirroring trigger is installed in us_007.
26	create table public.creator_profiles (
27	  user_id                     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
28	  display_name                text,
29	  bio                         text,
30	  country                     text,
31	  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
32	  tiktok_avg_views_last_10    integer,
33	  tiktok_total_likes          bigint,
34	  tiktok_video_count          integer,
35	  tiktok_is_verified          boolean,
36	  instagram_follower_count    integer,
37	  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer,
38	  instagram_media_count       integer,
39	  metrics_fetched_at          timestamptz,
40	  created_at                  timestamptz not null default now(),
41	  updated_at                  timestamptz not null default now()
42	);
43	create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
44	create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);
45	
46	-- ---- lister_profiles ----------------------------------------------------
47	create table public.lister_profiles (
48	  user_id     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
49	  org_name    text not null,
50	  website_url text,
51	  logo_path   text,
52	  description text,
53	  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
54	  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now()
55	);
56	
57	-- ---- social_links -------------------------------------------------------
58	-- Partial unique indexes release both the (platform, handle) reservation AND
59	-- the (user_id, platform) slot when status='unlinked', so a relink of the
60	-- same (or a different) handle does not collide with the historical row.
61	create table public.social_links (
62	  id                      uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
63	  user_id                 uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
64	  platform                public.platform not null,
65	  handle                  extensions.citext not null,
66	  status                  public.social_link_status not null default 'linked',
67	  last_scrape_run_id      text,
68	  last_scrape_attempt_at  timestamptz,
69	  last_scrape_error       text,
70	  fail_count              smallint not null default 0,
71	  handle_confirmed_at     timestamptz,
72	  created_at              timestamptz not null default now(),
73	  updated_at              timestamptz not null default now()
74	);
75	create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
76	  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';
77	create unique index social_links_platform_handle_uniq
78	  on public.social_links (platform, handle) where status <> 'unlinked';
79

1	-- US-005: listings + versioning + conditions + sample videos
2	-- Per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 blocks 7, 8, 9.
3	-- Story US-005 originally called for `listing_pre_conditions` + `listing_post_conditions`
4	-- but the canonical spec defines a single `listing_conditions` table discriminated by
5	-- the `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post'). The spec wins (see progress.txt Spec gap).
6	
7	-- =========================================================
8	-- 7. listings
9	-- =========================================================
10	create table public.listings (
11	  id                                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
12	  lister_id                         uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
13	  status                            listing_status not null default 'draft',
14	  title                             text not null,
15	  description                       text,
16	  category                          text not null default 'other',
17	  price_cents                       integer not null check (price_cents >= 0),
18	  currency                          text not null default 'USD',
19	  max_submissions                   integer,
20	  approved_submissions_count        integer not null default 0,
21	  active_pending_applications_count integer not null default 0,
22	  end_date                          timestamptz,
23	  current_version_id                uuid,
24	  version_number                    integer not null default 1,
25	  min_followers_tiktok              integer,
26	  min_followers_instagram           integer,
27	  version_bump_reason               text,
28	  created_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
29	  updated_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
30	  published_at                      timestamptz,
31	  closed_at                         timestamptz
32	);
33	
34	create index listings_feed_idx
35	  on public.listings (status, created_at desc) where status = 'active';
36	create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);
37	create index listings_tt_threshold_idx
38	  on public.listings (min_followers_tiktok) where status = 'active';
39	create index listings_ig_threshold_idx
40	  on public.listings (min_followers_instagram) where status = 'active';
41	create index listings_category_price_idx
42	  on public.listings (category, price_cents) where status = 'active';
43	
44	-- =========================================================
45	-- 7b. listing_versions
46	-- =========================================================
47	create table public.listing_versions (
48	  id                  uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
49	  listing_id          uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
50	  version_number      integer not null,
51	  price_cents         integer not null,
52	  currency            text not null,
53	  max_submissions     integer,
54	  snapshot            jsonb not null,
55	  previous_version_id uuid references public.listing_versions(id),
56	  changed_fields      text[] not null default '{}',
57	  created_at          timestamptz not null default now(),
58	  unique (listing_id, version_number)
59	);
60	create index listing_versions_listing_ver_desc_idx
61	  on public.listing_versions (listing_id, version_number desc);
62	
63	-- Close the circular FK: listings.current_version_id -> listing_versions.id
64	alter table public.listings
65	  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
66	  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);
67	
68	-- =========================================================
69	-- 8. listing_conditions (data-driven pre + post conditions)
70	-- =========================================================
71	create table public.listing_conditions (
72	  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
73	  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
74	  kind               condition_kind not null,
75	  metric             condition_metric not null,
76	  platform           platform,
77	  operator           text not null default 'gte'
78	    check (operator in ('gte','lte','eq','contains','bool')),
79	  numeric_threshold  numeric,
80	  text_threshold     text,
81	  bool_threshold     boolean,
82	  created_at         timestamptz not null default now()
83	);
84	create index listing_conditions_version_kind_idx
85	  on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);
86	
87	-- =========================================================
88	-- 9. sample_videos
89	-- =========================================================
90	create table public.sample_videos (
91	  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
92	  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
93	  platform           platform not null,
94	  url                text not null,
95	  caption            text,
96	  sort_order         smallint not null default 0
97	);
98	create index sample_videos_version_idx
99	  on public.sample_videos (listing_version_id);
100

1	-- US-007: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger.
2	--
3	-- Per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) and the trigger
4	-- definitions in §15 (lines 1205-1291).
5	--
6	-- Three pieces:
7	--   1. public.metric_snapshots — historized rows, one per Apify run.
8	--   2. app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence — BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE
9	--      trigger ensuring scrape_mode matches the linked social_link.platform.
10	--   3. app_private.denorm_metrics — BEFORE INSERT trigger that, for fresh
11	--      rows, demotes the previous latest under a (social_link, scrape_mode)
12	--      advisory lock and mirrors the owned columns into creator_profiles.
13	--
14	-- Spec gap (story text vs. spec DDL): the story acceptance criterion says the
15	-- advisory lock is keyed on (user_id, scrape_mode); the spec keys it on
16	-- (social_link_id, scrape_mode). The two are functionally equivalent because
17	-- social_links has a partial unique index on (user_id, platform). Following
18	-- the spec verbatim.
19	
20	-- =========================================================
21	-- 1. metric_snapshots table
22	-- =========================================================
23	create table public.metric_snapshots (
24	  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
25	  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
26	  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
27	  apify_run_id      text,
28	  status            metric_status not null,
29	  follower_count    integer,
30	  following_count   integer,
31	  total_likes       bigint,
32	  video_count       integer,
33	  avg_views_last_10 integer,
34	  is_verified       boolean,
35	  raw_payload       jsonb,
36	  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
37	  is_latest         boolean not null default false,
38	  error_message     text
39	);
40	
41	create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
42	
43	-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode); IG has at most two.
44	create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
45	  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;
46	
47	-- Idempotency for webhook redeliveries — same Apify run never inserts twice.
48	create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
49	  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
50	
51	-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans here. Partial keeps it sub-ms.
52	create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
53	  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';
54	
55	-- =========================================================
56	-- 2. Coherence trigger: scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform
57	-- =========================================================
58	create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
59	returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
60	declare p platform;
61	begin
62	  select platform into p from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
63	  if p is null then
64	    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
65	  end if;
66	  if (p = 'tiktok'    and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile')
67	  or (p = 'instagram' and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details', 'ig_posts')) then
68	    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
69	  end if;
70	  return new;
71	end $$;
72	
73	create trigger trg_metric_snapshots_coherence
74	  before insert or update of scrape_mode, social_link_id on public.metric_snapshots
75	  for each row execute function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence();
76	
77	-- =========================================================
78	-- 3. Race-free latest-row promotion + denormalization trigger
79	-- =========================================================
80	-- BEFORE INSERT so we can mutate new.is_latest; advisory lock serializes
81	-- concurrent webhook deliveries / manual refreshes for the same
82	-- (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
83	create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
84	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
85	declare
86	  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
87	  lock_key bigint;
88	begin
89	  if new.status <> 'fresh' then
90	    -- 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows never become latest, never denormalize.
91	    new.is_latest := false;
92	    return new;
93	  end if;
94	
95	  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
96	  perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);
97	
98	  update public.metric_snapshots
99	     set is_latest = false
100	   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
101	     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
102	     and is_latest;
103	
104	  new.is_latest := true;
105	
106	  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
107	
108	  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
109	    update public.creator_profiles
110	       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
111	           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
112	           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
113	           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
114	           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
115	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
116	           updated_at               = now()
117	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
118	
119	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
120	    update public.creator_profiles
121	       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
122	           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
123	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
124	           updated_at               = now()
125	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
126	
127	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
128	    update public.creator_profiles
129	       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
130	           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
131	           updated_at                  = now()
132	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
133	  end if;
134	
135	  return new;
136	end $$;
137	
138	create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
139	  before insert on public.metric_snapshots
140	  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();
141

1	-- US-010: listing-version cascade trigger
2	-- Implements §15 `bump_listing_version` + `request_listing_version_bump` per
3	-- docs/tech-architecture.md lines 1103-1203.
4	--
5	-- BEFORE UPDATE on public.listings:
6	--   * Detects scalar edits (price_cents, currency, max_submissions) via old/new diff.
7	--   * Detects conditions / sample_videos edits via the ephemeral `version_bump_reason`
8	--     column, set-and-reset by app_private.request_listing_version_bump before the edge
9	--     function commits. The trigger consumes the cue so it never persists to disk.
10	--   * When any versioned field changed AND new.status = 'active':
11	--       - Bumps `new.version_number`.
12	--       - Inserts a public.listing_versions row (snapshot = to_jsonb(new)).
13	--       - Swaps `new.current_version_id` to the new version's id.
14	--       - Cascade-cancels every pending application on this listing →
15	--         status = 'cancelled_listing_edit'.
16	--       - Emits a public.notifications row per affected creator
17	--         (kind = 'listing_version_changed').
18	--   * Title, description, category, and status-only edits DO NOT cascade.
19	--
20	-- Both functions follow the repo hardening pattern:
21	--   `security definer` + `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualified refs to every
22	--   user-defined table / type. `pg_catalog` functions (now(), array_length,
23	--   to_jsonb, jsonb_build_object, coalesce) are bare — implicit. Enum literals are
24	--   cast explicitly (`'active'::public.listing_status`) so coercion does not
25	--   depend on the caller-side search_path.
26	--
27	-- Spec gap: §15b threshold-maintenance triggers (`refresh_listing_thresholds`,
28	-- `refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump`) are intentionally NOT included in this
29	-- migration. Story AC is scoped to the cascade trigger only; the threshold
30	-- triggers will be needed before US-038 (discover feed eligibility filter) and
31	-- US-049-055 (create/edit campaign wizard), and should land in whichever of
32	-- those stories first needs the listings.min_followers_* cache to populate.
33	
34	create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
35	returns trigger
36	language plpgsql
37	security definer
38	set search_path = ''
39	as $$
40	declare
41	  versioned_changed boolean;
42	  new_version_id    uuid;
43	  prev_version_id   uuid;
44	  changed_fields    text[] := array[]::text[];
45	begin
46	  if old.price_cents is distinct from new.price_cents then
47	    changed_fields := changed_fields || 'price_cents';
48	  end if;
49	  if old.currency is distinct from new.currency then
50	    changed_fields := changed_fields || 'currency';
51	  end if;
52	  if old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions then
53	    changed_fields := changed_fields || 'max_submissions';
54	  end if;
55	
56	  -- Ephemeral cue set by request_listing_version_bump when listing_conditions
57	  -- or sample_videos have been edited. Consume it so it never persists.
58	  if new.version_bump_reason is not null then
59	    changed_fields := changed_fields || new.version_bump_reason;
60	    new.version_bump_reason := null;
61	  end if;
62	
63	  -- array_length of an empty array is NULL, so wrap in coalesce.
64	  versioned_changed := coalesce(array_length(changed_fields, 1), 0) > 0;
65	
66	  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active'::public.listing_status then
67	    prev_version_id    := old.current_version_id;
68	    new.version_number := old.version_number + 1;
69	
70	    insert into public.listing_versions (
71	      listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
72	      snapshot, previous_version_id, changed_fields
73	    )
74	    values (
75	      new.id, new.version_number, new.price_cents, new.currency, new.max_submissions,
76	      to_jsonb(new), prev_version_id, changed_fields
77	    )
78	    returning id into new_version_id;
79	
80	    new.current_version_id := new_version_id;
81	
82	    -- Cascade pending applications + emit notifications in one statement so we
83	    -- notify exactly the creators whose applications were cancelled.
84	    with cascaded as (
85	      update public.applications
86	         set status     = 'cancelled_listing_edit'::public.application_status,
87	             updated_at = now()
88	       where listing_id = new.id
89	         and status     = 'pending'::public.application_status
90	      returning creator_id
91	    )
92	    insert into public.notifications (user_id, kind, payload)
93	    select c.creator_id,
94	           'listing_version_changed'::public.notification_kind,
95	           jsonb_build_object(
96	             'listing_id',          new.id,
97	             'new_version',         new.version_number,
98	             'previous_version_id', prev_version_id,
99	             'new_version_id',      new_version_id,
100	             'changed_fields',      to_jsonb(changed_fields)
101	           )
102	      from cascaded c;
103	  end if;
104	
105	  return new;
106	end;
107	$$;
108	
109	create trigger trg_bump_listing_version
110	  before update on public.listings
111	  for each row execute function app_private.bump_listing_version();
112	
113	-- Helper the edge function calls AFTER committing listing_conditions /
114	-- sample_videos edits. Produces an UPDATE that fires trg_bump_listing_version
115	-- with the appropriate cue. Invalid reasons raise so callers can never smuggle
116	-- arbitrary strings into changed_fields.
117	create or replace function app_private.request_listing_version_bump(
118	  p_listing_id uuid,
119	  p_reason     text
120	)
121	returns uuid
122	language plpgsql
123	security definer
124	set search_path = ''
125	as $$
126	declare
127	  new_ver uuid;
128	begin
129	  if p_reason not in ('conditions', 'sample_videos') then
130	    raise exception 'invalid version bump reason: %', p_reason;
131	  end if;
132	
133	  update public.listings
134	     set version_bump_reason = p_reason,
135	         updated_at          = now()
136	   where id = p_listing_id
137	  returning current_version_id into new_ver;
138	
139	  return new_ver;
140	end;
141	$$;
142

content.text:
Now I have everything I need to deliver a complete review.

---

Reviewing `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql` against the existing migration chain and the US-012 acceptance criteria.

---

## Critical Issues

None found.

---

## Important Issues (Medium or higher - blocking)

### 1. `ig_details` row seeds `is_verified = true`, denorm trigger does not write it - Confidence: 85

This is not a bug in the seed itself, but it creates a misleading/wrong expected state that will cause the `execute_sql` verification step to silently pass with incorrect data.

The `ig_details` branch of `denorm_metrics()` in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120000_us_007_metric_snapshots.sql` (lines 119-125) writes only `instagram_follower_count`, `instagram_media_count`, and `metrics_fetched_at`. There is no `instagram_is_verified` column on `creator_profiles`, so `is_verified = true` seeded on the `ig_details` row is silently dropped. This is fine architecturally, but the acceptance criteria does not list `instagram_is_verified` in its expected denorm values, so this is consistent. No action needed here except awareness: the `true` value stored in `metric_snapshots.is_verified` for the IG row is never denormalized and is arguably misleading seed data (IG verification is not modeled). Low enough impact that this does not block, but worth noting.

### 2. `denorm_metrics` trigger uses `GREATEST(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` - seed ordering sensitivity - Confidence: 80

The three `metric_snapshots` inserts are in a single `VALUES` list (lines 117-122 of the seed). PostgreSQL executes multi-row `INSERT ... VALUES` rows sequentially, top to bottom, within the same statement. The `BEFORE INSERT` trigger fires per-row in declaration order. All three use `now()`, which is stable within the transaction, so `GREATEST(metrics_fetched_at, now())` will behave correctly regardless of which row fires first - `metrics_fetched_at` starts NULL, becomes `now()` on the first relevant insert, and subsequent calls return `now()` unchanged via `GREATEST`. This is safe. Not a bug.

However, the acceptance criteria states `metrics_fetched_at` is "set" without specifying a value. Since all three `fetched_at` values are `now()` and the trigger uses `GREATEST`, the final `metrics_fetched_at` will equal transaction time. This is correct.

### 3. US-010 cascade trigger interaction is sound, but the comment in the seed is subtly imprecise - Confidence: 80 (cosmetic, not blocking)

The seed comment on line 43-45 says the follow-up `UPDATE` "touches only `current_version_id`". The actual `UPDATE` at lines 78-80 does exactly that: `SET current_version_id = v_version_id WHERE id = v_listing_id`. The `bump_listing_version` trigger checks `old.price_cents IS DISTINCT FROM new.price_cents` etc. None of those fields change in this UPDATE, and `version_bump_reason` remains NULL. The trigger returns `new` unchanged. No cascade fires. The logic is correct.

---

## Spec Compliance Check

All AC items verified:

- 1 lister (`lister@seed.marketify.test`, role=`lister`): present, with `lister_profiles` row.
- 1 listing in `status=active` with pre-conditions (2 rows: `min_followers` on tiktok + instagram) and post-conditions (2 rows: `post_family_friendly` + `post_must_mention`): present.
- 1 creator with `tiktok_handle` (via `social_links` platform=`tiktok`) and `instagram_handle` (platform=`instagram`): present.
- 3 `metric_snapshots` rows covering all three `scrape_mode` values (`tiktok_profile`, `ig_details`, `ig_posts`): present.

Expected denorm column values cross-checked against `denorm_metrics()` trigger branches:

| Column | Expected | Source row | Trigger writes it | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `tiktok_follower_count` | 42000 | `tiktok_profile`.`follower_count=42000` | yes | OK |
| `tiktok_avg_views_last_10` | 18500 | `tiktok_profile`.`avg_views_last_10=18500` | yes | OK |
| `tiktok_total_likes` | 850000 | `tiktok_profile`.`total_likes=850000` | yes | OK |
| `tiktok_video_count` | 120 | `tiktok_profile`.`video_count=120` | yes | OK |
| `tiktok_is_verified` | false | `tiktok_profile`.`is_verified=false` | yes | OK |
| `instagram_follower_count` | 15000 | `ig_details`.`follower_count=15000` | yes | OK |
| `instagram_avg_views_last_10` | 6400 | `ig_posts`.`avg_views_last_10=6400` | yes | OK |
| `instagram_media_count` | 55 | `ig_details`.`video_count=55` | yes (`video_count` maps to `instagram_media_count`) | OK |
| `metrics_fetched_at` | set | all three rows use `now()` | yes | OK |

All FK references are valid: `social_links` rows exist before `metric_snapshots` inserts, `listing_versions` row exists before `listing_conditions` and `sample_videos` inserts, and the deferred `UPDATE` to set `current_version_id` happens after the version row is committed within the same `DO` block.

Enum values used:
- `role`: `lister`, `creator` - matches `user_role` enum from US-004.
- `status` on `social_links`: `linked` - matches `social_link_status` enum.
- `scrape_mode`: `tiktok_profile`, `ig_details`, `ig_posts` - matches `scrape_mode` enum.
- `status` on `metric_snapshots`: `fresh` - matches `metric_status` enum.
- `listing status`: `active` - matches `listing_status` enum.
- `kind` on `listing_conditions`: `pre`, `post` - matches `condition_kind` enum.
- `operator`: `gte`, `bool`, `contains` - all in the CHECK constraint from US-005.

---

## Verdict

No blocking bugs. The seed is spec-compliant and consistent with the existing migration chain. The one item worth a second look before merge (item 1 above) is that `is_verified = true` on the `ig_details` row is semantically odd (Instagram verification is not a modeled denorm column), but it causes no failure and the acceptance-criteria values are all correct. Safe to commit.
